step_by_step_thinking: I agree with setting the minimum age for osteoporosis screening at 50 years old. This approach helps identify high-risk individuals earlier, allowing for timely intervention which can significantly reduce future fractures and related complications. Given that many women experience menopause around this age, starting screenings then aligns closely with periods where bone density loss may accelerate due to hormonal changes. Therefore, my answer remains D - 50 years. Agree.
